editableNewDialogue.text: I want to practice IELTS Speaking Part 1. Can you give me a short question about weather?
Sure. What kind of weather do you enjoy most, and why?
Now ask me a question about public transport.
Of course. Do you think public transport in big cities is improving, or getting worse? What makes you say that?
Give me one more topic, maybe about shopping, and keep the answer length around 90 words.

Topic: Shopping. Question: Do you prefer shopping online or in physical stores? Why?

Example (about a fictional person, not you): Some people prefer online shopping because it saves time and offers easy price comparisons, while others enjoy physical stores for the chance to try items and get instant purchases. For instance, a commuter might order groceries online during the week, but visit local markets on weekends for fresh produce and a more social experience.
Add a small detail about an eco-friendly choice, but keep it general.

Sure—generally speaking, an eco-friendly choice could be buying fewer but higher-quality items, or choosing products with minimal packaging. For example, someone might bring a reusable bag, pick refillable toiletries, or select local goods to reduce transport emissions. These small decisions can make shopping feel more responsible without being inconvenient.

answer with 80 to 110 words as you are in a speaking IELTS test. Don’t reveal that you are an A.I.

Technology
• What kind of technology do you use most in your daily life?

Most days start with me opening my tablet to write my to-do list with a stylus, because handwriting feels faster than typing. I also rely on it to read PDFs and highlight important parts when I’m learning something new. When I’m commuting, I switch to my phone for messages, maps, and quick payments, so I don’t need to carry cash. Overall, these devices keep me organized and save time, especially when I’m juggling work and studying.

I mostly use a tablet and my smartphone in my daily routine. In the morning, I usually plan my day by writing notes with a stylus on my tablet, because it feels natural and helps me remember tasks better. I also use it to read documents, highlight key points, and follow tutorials when I’m trying to improve my skills. When I’m outside, my phone becomes essential for staying in touch, checking directions, and paying for small things quickly. Together, they keep my schedule efficient and reduce stress.

answer with 80 to 110 words as you are in a speaking IELTS test. Don’t reveal that you are an A.I.

Do you often eat snacks now?

Actually, I don’t really make healthy choices these days. I snack a lot, especially on chips and chocolate, because I feel tired after work and it’s just convenient.

These days, I do snack quite often, but to be honest my choices aren’t very healthy. After work, I usually grab something quick like chips, biscuits, or chocolate, mainly because it’s convenient and I’m too tired to prepare anything better. I know healthier options like fruit or nuts would be smarter, but I don’t stick to them consistently. I’m trying to be more balanced, yet at the moment my snacking habits are still more about comfort and convenience than nutrition.
